refnamediv.refpurpose: Given a geometry collection, returns a multi-geometry containing only elements of a specified type.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Given a geometry collection, returns a homogeneous multi-geometry.
If the type is not specified, returns a multi-geometry containing only geometries of the highest dimension. So polygons are preferred over lines, which are preferred over points.

If the type is specified, returns a multi-geometry containing only that type. If there are no sub-geometries of the right type, an EMPTY geometry is returned. Only points, lines and polygons are supported. The type numbers are:

For atomic geometry inputs, the geometry is returned unchanged if the input type matches the requested type. Otherwise, the result is an EMPTY geometry of the specified type. If required, these can be converted to multi-geometries using .

warning.para: MultiPolygon results are not checked for validity. If the polygon components are adjacent or overlapping the result will be invalid. (For example, this can occur when applying this function to an result.) This situation can be checked with and repaired with .

note.para: Prior to 1.5.3 this function returned atomic inputs unchanged, no matter type. In 1.5.3 non-matching single geometries returned a NULL result. In 2.0.0 non-matching single geometries return an EMPTY result of the requested type.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.programlisting: SELECT ST_AsText(ST_CollectionExtract( 'GEOMETRYCOLLECTION( POINT(0 0), LINESTRING(1 1, 2 2) )')); st_astext --------------- MULTILINESTRING((1 1, 2 2))
